content: Existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned around the language learning definition of reflexion as 'a reflection of one’s own understanding', framing LLM self-reflection not just as an error-correction step but as a dynamic memory update that mirrors how human language learners consolidate reflexive knowledge of their own reasoning capabilities. This unlocks a structured curriculum-style approach to improving LLM self-reflection performance, rather than only engineering ad-hoc perspective-taking prompts.
